scenario.context: Station 8 of the daily run: conclude availability, damage, and documentation.
scenario.mission: You connect quantitative assessment with clear security documentation for management.

station.profile: Downtime, damage impact, residual risk, and closing communication
constraints: Phrase the conclusion briefly and robustly

customerRequest.subject: Station 08: complete the security documentation for the department management
customerRequest.body: Please prepare a short conclusion with availability context, residual risk, and traceable security documentation for the department management.

questions.title: Good security documentation
questions.prompt: Which characteristics does good security documentation have? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Protection need, decisions, and rationales are recorded traceably
options.rationale: Without rationale, the statement loses value.

options.text: Open residual risks and next steps are named

options.rationale: Only then does implementation remain manageable.

options.text: Important content is intentionally omitted so it looks shorter
options.rationale: That makes the documentation lose its value.

options.text: Results are phrased understandably for management and the specialist department

feedback.correct: Correct: this is how documentation becomes a management tool.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: please weight traceability, residual items, and responsibility more strongly.

questions.title: Closing communication for management
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for clean closing communication.
items: Draft the closing note with status and residual risk
Record follow-up and the next date
correctOrder: Draft the closing note with status and residual risk
Record follow-up and the next date

feedback.correct: Correct: this creates a robust and manageable conclusion.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: please separate assessment, metrics, alignment, and conclusion more clearly.

questions.title: Handling residual risk
questions.prompt: A protection concept is feasible, but a small residual risk remains. What is the best response?

options.text: Document the residual risk transparently, name responsible people, and define a review
options.rationale: That keeps the risk visible and manageable.

options.text: Hide the residual risk so approval goes faster
options.rationale: That would be professionally and organizationally wrong.

options.text: Stop all measures even though the risk is small and manageable
options.rationale: Without further justification, that would be disproportionate.

feedback.correct: Correct: residual risks must remain visible and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: residual risks need transparency and steering.

questions.title: Downtime at 99.8% availability
questions.prompt: A service is expected to achieve 99.8% availability in a 30-day month. How many minutes of downtime are at most included?

feedback.wrong: Please multiply the total minutes of the month by the downtime share.

questions.title: Closing note for management
questions.prompt: In 4-6 sentences, write a closing note with protection need, availability reference, and residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: State the protection need, classify the metric, and connect the residual risk with the next step.

feedback.correct: Correct: the note is clear, concise, and suitable for management.
feedback.wrong: Please connect protection need, the metric, and residual risk more clearly.
